title: gnaphalium plantagineum
intro: Gnaphalium plantagineum, also known as Jerusalem tea or buttonweed, is a perennial herb in the daisy family native to southern Europe and western Asia. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine, where it has been valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as colds, fever, and skin irritations. Consuming the leaves of the plant has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Gnaphalium plantagineum can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
